codeReview.summary: The feature implementation matches the requested landing page and responsive app-wide header behavior (public landing hero/CTAs, signed-in-only nav links, active-link styling, auth-aware logo target, and mobile hamburger navigation). I did not find any high-confidence correctness defects in the reviewed diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: A key UI composition convention appears undocumented: this codebase's shadcn/base-ui setup uses `render` instead of Radix-style `asChild`. The worker initially implemented with the wrong pattern and then corrected it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows a discovery step (`Grep` for `asChild|render=`) followed by note 'base-ui uses `render` prop instead of `asChild`' and corrective edits to `src/app/page.tsx` and `src/components/header.tsx`. This convention is not called out in mission `AGENTS.md` or `.factory/library/*`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `fullstack-worker` skill says to run a failing red-phase test before implementation, but the transcript shows tests were first executed after implementation. This suggests either procedure drift or that the skill/checklist could better enforce evidencing TDD steps.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: In transcript order: `Create src/app/page.test.tsx` -> implement page/header -> first `Execute npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` occurs after implementation. Handoff still reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Reviewed feature `landing-page-and-layout` at commit `cd14e5c` using the handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit diff. Result: pass, with no high-confidence correctness issues found. Added two shared-state observations (UI composition convention documentation gap and TDD procedure/evidence mismatch).
